title: betula pubescens var. glabrata
intro: Betula pubescens var. glabrata, also known as the glabrous birch, is a variety of the white birch tree native to Europe and Asia. This deciduous tree is valued for its bark, which contains salicin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Betula pubescens var. glabrata include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the bark can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, offering various health benefits including soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and promoting wound healing.
